notes: Released 11 Feb 2026 for public comment (comments due 3 Mar 2026); NOT in force. DAP 2020 remains operative. The document's own internal index sheet refers to itself as "DAP 2025" in places (e.g. "Refers to Para 11 of Chapter I of DAP 2025") - likely a leftover working title from drafting; treat as the same document.

Filename reads "2011-11" but the MoD ID is dated 11.11.2021 (November 2021, not 2011) - filename appears to be a transposition error. First in a series of Business Process Re-engineering (BPR) amendments to DAP 2020 (MoD ID 1(8)/D(Acq)/21). Already incorporated into DAP-2020_after_BPR_Ph_V_01_APR_2024.pdf.
MoD ID 1(8)/D(Acq)/21 dated 13 Apr 2022. Covers weapons/platforms banned for import, among other changes. Already incorporated into DAP-2020_after_BPR_Ph_V_01_APR_2024.pdf.

Exact issue day is illegible in the source scan (MoD ID dated "..04.2023"); DAC approved the change 16 Mar 2023, used 1 Apr as a placeholder day - confirm against the original PDF if the exact date matters. Simplifies the Technology Development Fund (TDF) Scheme procedure (Chapter III, Para 75-79). Already incorporated into DAP-2020_after_BPR_Ph_V_01_APR_2024.pdf.

DAC approved 30.11.2023; amendment text states it is applicable from 15.01.2024, though the MoD ID itself is dated March 2024. Covers Indigenous Content requirement changes and Startup/MSME liberalisation. Already incorporated into DAP-2020_after_BPR_Ph_V_01_APR_2024.pdf.

DAC approved 16.02.2024; exact MoD ID issue day is illegible in the source scan, used 1 Apr as a placeholder - confirm against the original PDF if the exact date matters. Covers Defence Offset Guidelines (Appendix E, Chapter II) and Advanced Technology procurement from Startups/MSMEs. This is the phase referenced in DAP-2020_after_BPR_Ph_V_01_APR_2024.pdf's filename, which incorporates it and all four earlier phases.
Most current consolidated text of DAP 2020, incorporating all five BPR amendments above (Nov 2021 - Apr 2024). Recommended as the primary file to index/cite for DAP 2020 questions.

Original notification date not confirmed from the extracted text - 2020-01-01 is a placeholder, not a verified date. Near-duplicate of DAP_2020 _main.pdf and DAP2030new_0.pdf (same base text, only whitespace/line-wrap differences found between them); none include the 5 BPR amendments. Superseded in practice by DAP-2020_after_BPR_Ph_V_01_APR_2024.pdf. Recommend excluding from indexing to avoid duplicate/conflicting citations against the same paragraphs - retained for reference only.

Despite the filename, content confirms this is DAP 2020, not a document called "DAP 2030" - no such document exists among these files. Near-duplicate of DAP2020.pdf and DAP_2020 _main.pdf. Recommend excluding from indexing (see DAP2020.pdf notes).

Near-duplicate of DAP2020.pdf and DAP2030new_0.pdf (only whitespace/line-wrap differences found). Recommend excluding from indexing (see DAP2020.pdf notes) - of the three duplicates, any one could serve as an archival reference copy if needed.

doc_name: SOP - Confirmation of Foreign Bank Guarantees under DAP 2020
notes: Not a DAC-approved amendment per se - a Standard Operating Procedure supplementing Article 1.4.1 of Chapter VI and Para 1.3.5.1 of Appendix M, Schedule I to Chapter II. MoD ID 4(4)/D(Acq)/2A (Pt.) dated 14.09.2021.

doc_name: DFPDS 2026 (Delegation of Financial Powers to Defence Services)
notes: Companion document to DPM 2025 per its own foreword ("along with the Defence Procurement Manual (DPM) issued in 2025"); governs delegated financial powers/ceilings for procurement decision-making. Please confirm whether any capital-acquisition financial powers are also covered - the foreword frames it primarily as a revenue-procurement companion to DPM.
Dated 14 Oct 2025 on the cover; in force from 1 Nov 2025 per the DPM cutover rule. Covers Revenue Procurement chapters spanning definitions/process through RFP tendering contracts and indigenisation.
Companion volume to DPM 2025 Vol I; same effective date (1 Nov 2025).

MISFILED - this file is stored under DPM_2025 but its actual content is "DPM-2009 Amendment 1/2015" (dated 10.11.2015), not DPM 2025. Concerns exclusion of taxes/duties from L-1 (lowest bidder) evaluation criteria when only Indian bidders compete. Superseded along with all of DPM 2009 by DPM 2025 for RFPs issued on/after 1 Nov 2025; still governs any surviving pre-cutover RFP under the DPM cutover rule. Note: we do not have DPM 2009's main text in raw_pdfs, only this one amendment.

Predates and is superseded by DAP 2020 for capital acquisition. This consolidated copy states it incorporates amendments up to 1 Nov 2019 - the original DPP 2016 notification date is not confirmed in the extracted text.

Despite being filed under DPP 2016, this concerns DPP 2006, 2008 and 2011 (predates DPP 2016) - a DAC-approved SOP (29 Oct 2015 meeting) for processing offset contract amendment proposals. MoD ID dated 11.12.2015. Long superseded by DAP 2020 for current procurements.
Earlier consolidated snapshot of the same DPP 2016 text as DPP_2016_main.pdf; superseded by that later (01 Nov 2019) snapshot, which is itself superseded by DAP 2020.

doc_name: Draft Policy Guidelines - Simulation-Based Trial Evaluation (under DAP 2020)
notes: Internal office-order template with blank "No." and "Dated" fields - appears unsigned/unfinalised, not a notified policy. Elaborates on DAP 2020's simulation-in-trials provisions (Para 41 Schedule I to Chapter II, and others). Treat as draft/non-binding until confirmed otherwise.

doc_name: DAP 2020 Schedule I to Chapter XII - Shipbuilding RFP Format
notes: A blank standard-format RFP template (Schedule I to Chapter XII, refers to Para 73 of Chapter XII - Procedure for Defence Ship Building), not a specific real procurement case. Functions as an appendix/schedule of DAP 2020 rather than a standalone regulation.
